name: Darren Bailey
bio: Darren Bailey, born March 17, 1966, in Louisville, Illinois, is a farmer from Xenia, Clay County, who holds an Associate of Science in agricultural production from Lake Land College. He served one term in the Illinois House (109th District, 2019–2021) and one term in the Illinois Senate (55th District, 2021–2023) before becoming the 2022 Republican nominee for governor, losing to JB Pritzker by 12.5 percentage points (54.91% vs. 42.37%). After losing a 2024 congressional primary to incumbent Rep. Mike Bost by 2.8 points, Bailey announced a 2026 gubernatorial rematch in September 2025, pairing with Cook County Republican Chair Aaron Del Mar as running mate. He is campaigning on an 'Illinois DOGE' spending audit commission, repeal of the 2021 Climate and Equitable Jobs Act, and property tax reform, while moderating his tone following the October 2025 helicopter crash that killed his son Zachary, daughter-in-law Kelsey, and two grandchildren.
prior_office: Illinois Senate, 55th District (2021–2023); Illinois House, 109th District (2019–2021)

jacket.note: ILSBE Dec 31, 2025 quarterly report: $152,634 cash on hand; $316,885 in debts and obligations. FEC committee C00844449 ('Bailey for Congress') also registered — likely leftover from 2024 congressional primary — but shows 0 contributions in 2026 cycle, confirming governor race financing flows through ILSBE. Individual donor detail requires ILSBE PDF exhibit parsing; aggregate totals confirmed. Campaign finance reflects historically low fundraising for a gubernatorial candidate — far behind Ted Dabrowski's $1.8M. Committee formally confirmed via FEC committee lookup.

donors.name: Donors listed in ILSBE filing — itemized data not yet extracted from PDF exhibits

red_flags.label: Lost 2022 governor race by 12.5 points statewide
red_flags.detail: In the November 8, 2022 general election, Bailey received 1,739,095 votes (42.37%) vs. JB Pritzker's 2,253,748 (54.91%), a 12.5-percentage-point margin — the widest Illinois Republican loss in a governor's race since 2002. Bailey was shut out in all six collar counties except one, reflecting deep suburban Chicago weakness.

red_flags.label: Pritzker spent $24M via DGA to prop Bailey up in GOP primary
red_flags.detail: Gov. JB Pritzker contributed $24 million to the Democratic Governors' Association, which ran ads calling Bailey 'too conservative for Illinois' — a deliberate strategy to elevate the most beatable Republican over moderate Richard Irvin (backed by $50M from Ken Griffin). The tactic worked: Bailey won the June 2022 primary with 57.7%, then lost the general by 12.5 points.

red_flags.label: Voted to raise school property taxes 81% — now campaigns against property taxes
red_flags.detail: Tax records at the Clay County Treasurer's office and Illinois Department of Revenue show that from 1996 to 2012, while serving on the North Clay Board of Education, Bailey voted to increase the property tax levy every time one was proposed, totaling an 81% combined increase — a direct contradiction of his anti-property-tax legislative and campaign messaging.

red_flags.label: Least productive legislator in Senate GOP caucus: 1 bill signed in 2 years
red_flags.detail: As an Illinois state senator from 2021–2023, Bailey was chief sponsor of exactly one bill that became law — SB 2150, a firefighter recruitment measure. WBEZ found no member of the 18-person Senate Republican caucus had fewer chief-sponsored bills enacted. His House record was similarly thin: one bill (increased fines for passing school buses) over two years.

red_flags.label: Called for statewide abortion ban with no rape/incest exception
red_flags.detail: Bailey supports a complete statewide ban on abortion except to save the mother's life, explicitly opposing exceptions for rape or incest. In a 2017 Facebook video, he compared abortion to the Holocaust, stating 'the attempted extermination of the Jews of World War II doesn't even compare on a shadow of the life that has been lost with abortion since its legalization.'

red_flags.label: Refused to wear mask in 2020 House session, ejected in 81-27 bipartisan vote
red_flags.detail: On May 20, 2020, the Illinois House voted 81–27 — a bipartisan margin — to remove Bailey from the chamber floor for refusing to wear a mask during the COVID-19 pandemic. He had also filed a lawsuit in Clay County Circuit Court challenging Pritzker's stay-at-home order; a judge issued a temporary restraining order that applied only to Bailey personally and was later vacated.

red_flags.label: Highland Park shooting 'move on' gaffe caused national backlash
red_flags.detail: On July 4, 2022, within 90 minutes of the Highland Park parade shooting that killed 7 people, Bailey stated in a Facebook livestream: 'The shooter is still at large, so let's pray for justice to prevail, and then let's move on and let's celebrate the independence of this nation.' He later apologized. The incident reinforced his image as insensitive to suburban Chicago communities.

key_votes.summary: The SAFE-T Act abolished mandatory cash bail for criminal defendants in Illinois and implemented broader criminal justice reforms. Bailey voted against it and later made opposition to the Act a cornerstone of his tough-on-crime gubernatorial campaigns.
Bailey voted against legalizing recreational cannabis in Illinois, consistent with his socially conservative position opposing the bill signed by Gov. Pritzker in June 2019.
Bailey voted against the Illinois casino expansion bill that authorized new casinos including a potential Chicago casino. He opposed further gambling expansion on moral grounds.

Bailey voted against the $45 billion Rebuild Illinois infrastructure package, the state's largest such investment in decades, which funded roads, bridges, schools, and transit through a gas tax increase.

Bailey sponsored a resolution calling for Chicago to be expelled from Illinois and made its own state, citing policy disagreements on guns, abortion, and immigration. It gained no traction legislatively. Bailey later called it 'a warning shot' after it became a liability during the 2022 governor race.

key_votes.bill: HB 3831 (Gender Reassignment Surgery Funding Ban) – 101st GA
key_votes.summary: Bailey pushed legislation to prohibit public funding for gender reassignment surgery, part of a broader pattern of anti-transgender legislation he championed during his House term.

The only bill Bailey successfully shepherded into law as a state senator, SB 2150 created incentives to recruit volunteer firefighters. His entire two-year Senate record produced only this one enacted bill, according to WBEZ's review.

Bailey vocally and repeatedly opposed the Reproductive Health Act, Illinois's landmark 2019 abortion-rights law that repealed the state's dormant abortion ban and established abortion as a fundamental right. He challenged the bill's use of 'individual' rather than 'woman,' and has since called for a complete abortion ban in Illinois.

endorsements: Donald Trump (endorsed in 2022 primary; Bailey not seeking Trump endorsement in 2026 race)
Dick Uihlein, Uline founder — $18M+ in 2022 (no equivalent 2026 endorsement confirmed)
policy_positions: Proposes 'Illinois DOGE' efficiency commission led by Lt. Gov. candidate Aaron Del Mar to audit state boards, commissions, and duplicate agencies — no specific dollar savings estimate given
Repeal 2021 Climate and Equitable Jobs Act (CEJA); return to fossil fuels + nuclear energy mix to lower utility rates
Cap annual property tax rates to not exceed a homeowner's mortgage rate; state to compensate with increased education funding (mechanism unspecified)
Repeal TRUST Act (state sanctuary law preventing local police cooperation with ICE); stated he would pursue legislative consensus rather than executive order
Support immigrants becoming naturalized citizens; distinguish from illegal immigration enforcement
Requires independent audits of major utility contracts
School choice expansion; end Chicago as 'political battleground' in education
Running as more moderate than 2022; distancing from Trump endorsement

career_history.role: Farmer and Private Christian School Operator
career_history.org: Bailey Family Farm; Xenia, IL private school
career_history.highlight: Multi-generational family farm in Clay County. He and wife Cindy also operate a private Christian school in Xenia.
Voted to raise property tax levy by a combined 81% across his tenure — later became a liability when campaigning against property taxes.

career_history.role: Member, Illinois House of Representatives (109th District)
career_history.highlight: Sponsored Chicago expulsion resolution (HR 101), opposed Reproductive Health Act, cannabis legalization, and major infrastructure package. One enacted bill (school bus fine increase) in two years.
Voted against SAFE-T Act (cash bail abolition). Sponsored one enacted bill in two years (firefighter recruitment). Served on Agriculture, Education, Health, and Energy committees.
Won 6-way primary with 57.7% (aided by $24M Pritzker/DGA ad campaign). Lost general election to JB Pritzker 42.37% to 54.91% — a 12.5-point loss, with collapse in suburban Chicago collar counties.
Lost Republican primary to incumbent Mike Bost by 2.8 percentage points (48.6% vs. 51.4%) despite Trump endorsing Bost.

trust_indicators.label: Legislative record
trust_indicators.value: 2 total bills enacted across 4-year career (House + Senate combined) — fewest of any Senate Republican in his caucus

trust_indicators.label: 2022 general election loss
trust_indicators.value: Lost by 12.5 points statewide; swept in all major suburban counties

trust_indicators.label: Primary meddling
trust_indicators.value: Won 2022 primary partly through $24M in Democratic opposition ads strategically boosting his candidacy

trust_indicators.label: Campaign fundraising 2026
trust_indicators.value: Only $152,634 cash on hand as of Dec 31, 2025; $316,885 in debts — far behind Dabrowski's $1.8M raised

trust_indicators.label: Personal tragedy
trust_indicators.value: Son Zachary, daughter-in-law Kelsey, and two grandchildren (ages 12 and 7) killed in October 2025 Montana helicopter crash; Bailey described as a changed candidate since

trust_indicators.label: Republican frontrunner 2026
trust_indicators.value: Emerson College poll showed Bailey leading GOP primary field in early 2026 on name recognition from 2022 campaign

news_hits.headline: Familiar foes line up in Republican primary for chance against Gov. JB Pritzker
news_hits.summary: Chicago Sun-Times profiles Bailey as the field's frontrunner on name recognition from his 2022 loss, but notes the campaign has only $152K cash on hand vs. $316K in debts, and that he remained in the race after the October 2025 helicopter crash that killed his son, daughter-in-law, and two grandchildren — describing him as a 'changed candidate' running with a moderating tone.

news_hits.headline: Illinois primary election: Key races for governor, U.S. Senate
news_hits.summary: USA Today pre-primary analysis notes a Victory Research poll showing Pritzker with a 20% lead over Bailey in a hypothetical general election rematch, with Pritzker's approval at 52% — underscoring Bailey's continued electability challenge despite his frontrunner status in the GOP primary.

news_hits.headline: Bailey to make stop in Metropolis ahead of Illinois Primary
news_hits.title: Bailey to make stop in Metropolis ahead of Illinois Primary
news_hits.summary: KFVS12 (March 12) reports Bailey is making a southern Illinois campaign stop in Metropolis ahead of the March 17 primary, facing three other Republicans. Focuses on pocketbook issues rather than the social conservatism that defined his 2022 campaign.

news_hits.headline: Illinois 2026 GOP primary for governor features four candidates and one familiar face but big donors sit out
news_hits.title: Illinois 2026 GOP primary for governor features four candidates and one familiar face but big donors sit out
news_hits.summary: Yahoo/AP reports Bailey is running his 2026 campaign without major outside donor support, telling voters he is 'running for governor of Illinois alone, without any outside influence.' Notes he shifted from social conservative themes to economic ones.

news_hits.title: Illinois primary election: Key races for governor, U.S. Senate, comptroller
news_hits.summary: Day-before-primary overview (March 14) of all key Illinois races. Covers JB Pritzker's uncontested Democratic primary for governor and Republican primary field of Bailey, Dabrowski, Heidner, and Mendrick. Also covers Senate race and comptroller.

news_hits.title: On final weekend before Illinois primary, candidates try to keep it local despite flood of out-of-state money
news_hits.summary: Tribune final-weekend dispatch describes the four-way GOP governor's primary: Bailey (downstate farmer, lost to Pritzker by 13 points in 2022), Dabrowski, Heidner, and Mendrick. Bailey is focusing on pocketbook issues over the social hot-buttons that defined his 2022 run.

news_hits.title: Illinois primary: What to know for Tuesday's election
news_hits.summary: Tribune day-before guide covers the four-way Republican governor's primary. Notes Bailey as the downstate farmer and former state lawmaker who won the 2022 GOP primary but lost to Pritzker by nearly 13 points — now running again with a pocketbook-focused message.

news_hits.title: Five Illinois congressional seats, Senate seat open in historic election year
news_hits.summary: WBEZ comprehensive primary preview confirms Darren Bailey is running in the four-way GOP governor's primary alongside Dabrowski, Heidner, and Mendrick, all competing for the right to face incumbent Gov. JB Pritzker in the November general.

news_hits.title: What to expect in the Illinois state primary
news_hits.summary: AP Decision Notes preview of the March 17 Illinois primary. Senate race: Kelly, Krishnamoorthi, and Stratton (Pritzker-endorsed) top the Democratic field; Krishnamoorthi leads in fundraising with ~$6.6M cash on hand. Pritzker is unopposed for Democratic governor renomination. Republican governor field includes multiple challengers trying to prevent Pritzker's third term.

news_hits.title: 4 things to watch Tuesday in Illinois primaries, including a hotly contested Senate battle
news_hits.summary: STLPR election-eve preview notes Bailey is the frontrunner in the four-way GOP governor's primary, but questions whether DuPage County Sheriff James Mendrick — who has broader suburban Chicago appeal — can upset him. GOP voters must decide whether electability or ideology matters more. Pritzker is running uncontested on the Democratic side.

news_hits.title: 2026 Illinois primary election voter guide
news_hits.summary: Voter guide covering Bailey's Republican gubernatorial primary. Bailey previously lost to Pritzker 42-55% as the 2022 GOP nominee; now seeking the party's nod again against Aaron B. Del Mar and others.

news_hits.title: Can anyone beat Darren Bailey in the Republican primary for Illinois governor?
news_hits.summary: Illinois Republicans decide whether to run back Darren Bailey (who lost to Pritzker in 2022) or send a new challenger to face Pritzker in November.

news_hits.title: Illinois governor primary election results 2026
news_hits.summary: Darren Bailey, a former state senator who lost to Pritzker in 2022, is among Republicans seeking the GOP gubernatorial nomination again.

news_hits.title: Election Night: Bailey competing in Republican governor primary to face Pritzker
news_hits.summary: Former state Sen. Darren Bailey — who lost to Gov. Pritzker by 11 points in the 2022 general election — is competing in the Republican gubernatorial primary on March 17. Four Republican candidates are on the ballot looking to thwart Pritzker's bid for a rare third term. Pritzker ran unopposed on the Democratic side and addressed supporters after polls closed, vowing to fight Trump's agenda.

news_hits.title: Darren Bailey wins rematch bid against JB Pritzker in Illinois governor race
news_hits.summary: Tribune: Bailey and running mate Aaron Del Mar celebrated the Republican gubernatorial nomination at their election night party in Springfield. In his victory speech, Bailey said Illinois has a 'leadership problem, and nowhere is that clearer than in property taxes.'

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton, Daniel Biss, Darren Bailey among winners in 2026 Illinois primary elections
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times election night wrap: 'Darren Bailey cruised to victory for the Illinois Republican nomination for governor, breezing past conservative activist Ted Dabrowski, video gambling magnate Rick Heidner and DuPage County Sheriff James Mendrick.'

news_hits.title: Darren Bailey Wins Second Chance to Challenge Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker
news_hits.summary: The New York Times reports AP has called the Republican nomination for governor for Darren Bailey, a Trump-aligned former state legislator who lost the 2022 race to Pritzker 55%-42%. Bailey will get a rematch against Pritzker in the November general election.

news_hits.title: Republicans' chances of flipping Illinois governor after Darren Bailey wins primary
news_hits.summary: Newsweek analyzes GOP chances of flipping the Illinois governorship after Bailey wins the Republican primary. Notes he faces heavy structural disadvantages in the deep-blue state against incumbent JB Pritzker, but some Republicans point to national anti-incumbent headwinds as a potential opportunity.

news_hits.title: Who won the 2026 Illinois primary election? Latest results, and which races remain too close to call
news_hits.summary: NBC Chicago post-primary roundup confirms Bailey won the Republican gubernatorial nomination and will face incumbent Gov. Pritzker in a November rematch. The result gives Illinois Republicans their nominee — but the general election against the well-funded incumbent faces a steep climb in a state where Democrats hold a structural advantage.

news_hits.title: Editorial: Illinois Republicans are disappearing
news_hits.summary: Tribune editorial notes Illinois Republican primary turnout collapsed — Bailey and his three rivals collectively drew fewer votes than even the historically low 2014 primary, raising questions about party viability heading into the general election.

news_hits.title: How Illinois voted in the Republican primary for governor
news_hits.summary: Tribune post-primary geographic breakdown of Bailey's Republican governor primary victory. The November election will feature a rematch of the 2022 general election: incumbent Democratic Gov. JB Pritzker vs. Republican challenger Darren Bailey. Bailey won with 48% of the vote in a multi-candidate field including Ted Dabrowski, Rick Heidner, and James Mendrick.

news_hits.title: Illinois Governor Primary 2026: Darren Bailey clinches Republican nomination
news_hits.summary: Darren Bailey, a former state legislator aligned with President Trump, ran up huge margins in rural downstate counties to clinch the Republican nomination. He will now face Gov. JB Pritzker in the November general election.

news_hits.title: Democratic Gov. JB Pritzker faces Darren Bailey, winner of four-way Republican primary
news_hits.summary: NBC News confirms Bailey as Republican nominee for governor, setting up a general election rematch-style contest against incumbent Pritzker, who seeks a third term ahead of a possible 2028 presidential run.

news_hits.title: Stratton victory boosts Pritzker clout — Pritzker targets Bailey immediately after primary
news_hits.summary: Politico Illinois Playbook morning-after briefing notes Pritzker was immediately out with ads targeting Bailey, the Republican gubernatorial nominee, the morning after the primary. Pritzker's campaign is framing the November governor's race as a sharp contrast on abortion rights, education funding and economic policy against Bailey, who lost to Pritzker by 11 points in 2022.

news_hits.title: Illinois governor's race will be a rematch in 2026
news_hits.summary: Capitol News Illinois reports that Republican voters chose Darren Bailey to face Gov. JB Pritzker in a 2026 rematch, as Bailey secured the GOP gubernatorial nomination over three primary opponents — Ted Dabrowski, Rick Heidner, and James Mendrick. Bailey celebrates with running mate Aaron Del Mar after securing the nomination.

news_hits.title: Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker to face Republican Darren Bailey as he seeks a third term
news_hits.summary: NBC News confirms the general election matchup: Darren Bailey vs. JB Pritzker. The piece notes Illinois has proven difficult terrain for Republicans, who have been locked out of all statewide offices for eight years. Democrats hold supermajorities in both legislative chambers.

news_hits.title: More than $30M in outside spending shaped Illinois primaries, but wins were a split for big-monied interests
news_hits.summary: Post-primary spending analysis notes Bailey's primary win came with minimal outside PAC support — a contrast to the 2022 cycle where Pritzker spent $35M boosting Bailey in the GOP primary. In 2026, major Republican donors largely sat out the primary, leaving Bailey to run on his own political brand heading into what analysts expect will be another heavy underdog race against Pritzker.
